title: Calonyction macranthum
taxonomy: Calonyction macranthum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory family. It is a member of the genus Calonyction, which is a small group of tropical morning glory plants. The genus Calonyction is further grouped into the tribe Ipomoeae, which includes other species of morning glory and related plants. The classification of Calonyction macranthum is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular studies. This classification is widely accepted in the botanical community and is used to identify and distinguish this species from others.

intro: Calonyction macranthum, also known as the "red passionflower," is a flowering plant native to South America, characterized by its showy, trumpet-shaped flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, with reported health benefits including reduced stress levels and improved sleep quality.
